subject: Alaska.Ai — LinkedIn Carousel No. 3 — 2026-07-10 — The Machine That Hears a Mountain Think
html_body:
Alaska.Ai · LinkedIn Carousel No. 3

2026-07-10 · 9 slides · branch tsturg/serene-wright-avbkiz

1 · Upload the document

Download carousel.pdf (4.98 MB, vector text) → LinkedIn → start a post → add a document → upload the PDF → title it: The Machine That Hears a Mountain Think

2 · Paste the post copy

Three Alaska volcanoes are on elevated alert at once, and an AI built in Fairbanks is what stands watch over them.

Great Sitkin is erupting lava. Kupreanof and Shishaldin are stirring, and about 50,000 people fly over that airspace every day.

This deck walks through the two machine senses doing the watching. VOISS-Net is a neural ear, trained on more than 270,000 spectrograms to read tremor at 87 percent accuracy. VolcSARvatory is a satellite eye that catches a centimeter of ground swell from orbit. Both were built at UAF and are now scaling to all five US volcano observatories.

Kupreanof has no ground sensors at all, so is a machine watching from orbit really enough?

#AlaskaVolcanoes #VolcanoMonitoring #GeoAI

3 · Post, then paste this as the FIRST comment (within a minute)

Sources for today's deck.

- USGS Alaska Volcano Observatory, July 3 2026 weekly update (Great Sitkin Watch/Orange; Kupreanof and Shishaldin Advisory/Yellow; Kupreanof has no real-time network) https://volcanoes.usgs.gov/hans-public/notice/DOI-USGS-AVO-2026-07-03T18:33:40+00:00
- Volcanica, "A generalized deep learning model to detect and classify volcano seismicity" (VOISS-Net, more than 270,000 spectrograms, 87 percent accuracy, 7 volcanoes across 3 regions) https://www.jvolcanica.org/ojs/index.php/volcanica/article/view/349
- UAF Geophysical Institute, "Alaska-developed volcano monitoring system will expand across US" (VolcSARvatory, InSAR to 1 cm, Edgecumbe swarm April 11 2022, five USGS observatories, shared NASA award) https://www.gi.alaska.edu/news/alaska-developed-volcano-monitoring-system-will-expand-across-us
- USGS Alaska Volcano Observatory overview (130+ volcanoes, 50+ active since about 1760, 50,000 people a day overhead) https://www.usgs.gov/observatories/avo

Every decision and deadline we track lives at alaskaaihq.com.

See something we missed or read a slide differently? Add it in a reply.

Editor's note

Ship-worthy at 8.96 vs the 8.3 threshold with zero hard fails. Three honesty items before posting. (1) Slide 2's bottom trace is labeled COMBINED LIVE SEISMIC FEED but is illustrative art, not real waveform data. (2) Slide 6 says 1 cm per fringe as a stylized calibration; the verified claim is that the system detects movement as small as 1 cm. (3) Slide 7's Shishaldin elevation reads 'not listed' deliberately (unverifiable this run; honest omission). Inline previews in this email use raw GitHub URLs on main; if images do not load, tap 'display images'.

Automation changes this run (2)

The routine modified its own machinery this run. If a later week looks worse, this dated section is the rollback trail (each set reverts as one commit).
KindChangeRollback
fix
engine		qa.py now emits a WARN-level 'busy art under text' tripwire: for each PRIMARY text line box (font_px>=30) it samples the rendered PNG, masks the glyph ink (plus a 2px dilation), and warns when the remaining background carries high-contrast structured edges (bg edge density >= 0.03) -- i.e. a canvas/bitmap arc or texture crossing text that the DOM-only collision gate cannot see. WARN only; it points the pixel critics at a box and never FAILs.

Exits 1 with the offending line. Phase 3.5 and Phase 6 of the routine now lint the docket note and copy.json emitted fields with it BEFORE ship.
why: Run 2026-07-10 repeat friction: site_build's prose-colon ship gate fired TWICE in Phase 11 (a docket history note, then copy.json first_comment 'Sources for today's deck:'), forcin		revert the upgrade(2026-07-10) commit

Aftercare (this is where reach is won)

  • Post Tue to Thu, 8 to 11am AKT (drafts arrive daily; you own the posting cadence).
  • Paste the first-comment sources block within 60 seconds of posting.
  • Reply substantively to every comment in the first 90 minutes (golden hour drives up to 2.4x reach).
  • Check saves and comments at 48 hours against our trailing median, weighting saves over comments over likes.
  • Note for comments: S2's bottom trace is illustrative art (not real waveform data), S6's '1 cm per fringe' is a stylized calibration of the verified 1 cm detection floor, and S7's Shishaldin elevation reads 'not listed' by design (unverifiable this run).
